intended_use: Salix Central is a web-based software application that is intended to be used for viewing, post-processing, and analyzing cardiac computed tomography (CT) images acquired from a CT scanner in a Digita
intended_use_full: Salix Central is a web-based software application that is intended to be used for viewing, post-processing, and analyzing cardiac computed tomography (CT) images acquired from a CT scanner in a Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine (DICOM) Standard format. This software provides tools that can be used for the qualitative and quantitative assessment of physician-identified coronary plaques and stenosis in coronary computed tomography angiography (CCTA) and to perform calcium scoring in non-contrast cardiac CT.
target_condition: Coronary artery disease (CAD) and suspected CAD; evaluation of atherosclerotic plaque and stenosis in coronary vessels
ground_truth: Manual annotation and segmentation by board-certified cardiologists and radiologists with SCCT Level 3 certification (or equivalent experience) using manual annotation tools; independently established from source clinical image interpretation
other_metrics: Calcium Scoring - Pearson Correlation: 0.958 [0.947, 0.966]; Centerline Extraction - True Placement Percentage: 90.4% [88.5%, 92.2%]; Vessel Labelling - F1 Score: 78.4% [96.1%, 80.5%]; Lumen Wall Segmentation - Dice Score: 0.8996 (0.8938, 0.9055); Vessel Wall Segmentation - Dice Score: 0.9016 (0.8962, 0.9070)
